blurb: OpenAI's policy arm is in standards-building mode (the Appia Foundation conformity-specs play). Separately it remains a defendant in the consolidated NYT/news-publisher copyright litigation, where a Jan 2026 discovery ruling compels production of 20M ChatGPT logs.

publications.title: Helping build shared standards for advanced AI
publications.summary: OpenAI announces it helped found the Appia Foundation, hosted by the Linux Foundation, to develop open, modular specifications translating international standards into practical assessment criteria across the AI value chain. The framing: a missing 'trust layer' so third parties can check conformity and produce reusable evidence when models, infrastructure and apps come from different orgs. Ties back to OpenAI's earlier 'blueprint for democratic governance of frontier AI' (which calls for a strengthened US CAISI) and its CAISI/UK-AISI testing partnerships. The posture is unmistakable: OpenAI wants to shape the interoperable standards layer, not just comply with it.

controversies.title: New York Times & news publishers v OpenAI/Microsoft — copyright infringement (SDNY)
controversies.summary: OpenAI (with Microsoft) is a defendant in the consolidated copyright suits brought by the New York Times and other news publishers in the Southern District of New York. In March 2025 Judge Sidney Stein narrowed but allowed the core copyright-infringement claims to proceed past OpenAI's motion to dismiss. On 5 January 2026 Judge Stein affirmed a magistrate's discovery order compelling OpenAI to produce a 20-million-record sample of ChatGPT conversation logs to the plaintiffs, over OpenAI's privacy objections. The case is unresolved; OpenAI maintains an AI-training-as-fair-use defence. Reported as ongoing — no final merits ruling.

notes: policyPageUrl https://openai.com/news/global-affairs/ confirmed active. blogRssUrl in seed (openai.com/blog/rss.xml) is stale; working feed is https://openai.com/news/rss.xml. Controversy attribution: named-party (OpenAI is defendant), same-entity (the ChatGPT maker = seed openai-london); Reuters 401'd (paywall/JS gate) so cited NPR (named, free) rather than OpenAI's own /new-york-times/ PR page. Reuters body NOT reproduced per ToS.

news.title: OpenAI staggers new model release at US government request
news.summary: OpenAI is staggering the release of its latest model after a US-government request (echoing the Anthropic Mythos export-control move), and signalled dissatisfaction — saying it keeps the best tools from users, developers and global partners. A live example of frontier-model release becoming a national-security lever. Corroborated by CNBC.

news.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
news.summary: OpenAI Global Affairs post on supporting the EU’s trustworthy-AI ecosystem work — first-party signal on how the company is positioning around the EU AI Act and European governance.

news.title: PRC-linked influence operations (Global Affairs)
news.summary: OpenAI Global Affairs disclosure on PRC-linked influence operations — part of the lab’s threat-intelligence/transparency reporting, relevant to election-integrity and foreign-influence policy.
